label: Postgres + Drizzle
summary: Use when a Next.js app needs a server-side PostgreSQL database layer with Drizzle ORM, migrations, a typed schema module, shared DB client, and a simple health-check route. When DATABASE_URL is missing the app must not crash: pages fall back to static seedData with a discreet config notice, and API routes answer 503. Keep it to persistence infrastructure; application tables and queries should be adapted to the generated app domain.

envVars.purpose: PostgreSQL connection string used by the Drizzle client, database health route, and Drizzle Kit migrations. Get it from the Postgres provider, preferably a pooled connection URL. Tagged feature-runtime because the dossier degrades gracefully without it: isDbConfigured() gates all access, pages render seedData + DbConfigNotice, and /api/health/db returns 503.

notes: Core database dossier (default for capability 'database'). Schema + seed data are rewrite targets for the app domain. Legacy import wave 2: lazy getDb()/getPool() (never module-level), schema path fixed for v2 emission, users/posts example schema removed, F2 seed-fallback contract added. lastVerified carries the legacy source's date until the first preview/generate-test selects this dossier (no-false-green, same rule as PR #422/#430).
